chief_complaint: A 45-year-old woman presented with sudden vision loss and facial pain.
history: The patient had a history of poorly controlled diabetes mellitus complicated by diabetic ketoacidosis. She developed rhino-orbital-cerebral mucormycosis, refractory to systemic and local antifungal treatment as well as repeated extensive sinonasal debridement.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 37.5°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, pulse 100/min. Physical examination revealed decreased visual acuity, facial pain, and nasal discharge.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: elevated blood glucose, leukocytosis. Imaging: CT scan of the orbits showing mucormycosis. Other tests: negative galactomannan antigen.
